content.text:
<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. The user's task was clearly defined in a detailed multi-step plan to rename all I/O and system variable names in ST (Structured Text) files from P-address style names (P00000, P00001, etc.) to XG5000 auto-generated names (_00_IN00, _00_IN01, etc.).

2. Source files:
   - Variable mapping: `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\SHINJIN\1 CELL 조립 장비\Project\VAR.txt`
   - ST files to modify: All .st files in `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\SHINJIN\1 CELL 조립 장비\PLC RAW\ST_XEC\`
   - GVL_IO.st needed to be simplified

3. Steps taken:
   a. Read VAR.txt - contains XG5000 auto-generated variable names with their AT% addresses
   b. Read GVL_IO.st - contains current IX_/QX_ named variables with AT% addresses and P-address comments
   c. Listed all .st files in the directory
   d. Read all ST program files (ST_01 through ST_20)
   e. Grepped for all P-address, F-address, and U0A patterns in ST files
   f. Created a Python script (rename_vars.py) with complete mapping table
   g. First attempt to run script failed due to heredoc quoting issues in bash
   h. Wrote the script to a file instead and ran it successfully
   i. 1041 replacements across 17 files, with zero remaining P/F/U0A patterns
   j. Rewrote GVL_IO.st to be a reference-only comment file

Key mapping details:
- Slot 0 IN: P00000-P0001F → _00_IN00-_00_IN31
- Slot 0 OUT: P00020-P0003F → _00_OUT00-_00_OUT31
- Slot 1 IN: P00040-P0005F → _01_IN00-_01_IN31
- Slot 2 IN: P00080-P0009F → _02_IN00-_02_IN31
- Slot 3 IN: P00120-P0013F → _03_IN00-_03_IN31
- Slot 4 IN: P00160-P0017F → _04_IN00-_04_IN31
- Slot 5 OUT: P00200-P0021F → _05_OUT00-_05_OUT31
- Slot 6 OUT: P00240-P0025F → _06_OUT00-_06_OUT31
- Slot 7 OUT: P00280-P0029F → _07_OUT00-_07_OUT31
- Slot 8 OUT: P00320-P0033F → _08_OUT00-_08_OUT31
- F00093 → _T1S, F00099 → _ON, F0009A → _OFF
- U0A_xx → _0A_xx (analog module)

The task appears to be complete - all renames were applied successfully.
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a comprehensive rename of ALL I/O and system variable names across ST (Structured Text) files in an XG5000 XEC PLC project. The goal was to convert from XBC P-address style names (P00000, P00001, etc.) to XG5000 auto-generated naming conventions (_00_IN00, _00_IN01, etc.). This involved:
   - Building a complete mapping table from VAR.txt (XG5000 names + AT% addresses) and GVL_IO.st (P-address + AT% addresses)
   - Replacing all P-address variables (P00000~P0033F) in ST program files
   - Replacing F-system flags (F00093→_T1S, F00099→_ON, F0009A→_OFF)
   - Replacing U0A analog module variables (U0A_xx→_0A_xx)
   - Updating GVL_IO.st since I/O variables are auto-registered by XG5000
   - NOT renaming D, M, K, T variables (only P, U0A, F system flags)
   - NOT modifying GVL_INTERNAL.st, GVL_DATA.st, GVL_TIMER.st, GVL_HMI.st

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - LS Electric XEC-DN64H PLC (XG5000 IDE)
   - IEC 61131-3 Structured Text (ST) programming
   - XBC P-address to XEC AT% address mapping (e.g., P00000 → %IX0.0.0 → _00_IN00)
   - XG5000 auto-generated global/direct variable naming convention: _SS_INnn / _SS_OUTnn
   - Slot-based I/O architecture (Slots 0-8 for I/O, Slot 9 positioning, Slot 10 analog)
   - System flags: %FX147 (_T1S), %FX153 (_ON), %FX154 (_OFF)
   - Positioning module (U09): _09_RDY, _09_X_JOG_CW, etc.
   - Analog input module (U0A/Slot 10): _0A_ERR, _0A_RDY, _0A_CH0_DATA, etc.

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\SHINJIN\1 CELL 조립 장비\Project\VAR.txt`
     - Source of XG5000 auto-generated variable names with AT% addresses
     - 342 I/O variables (lines 1-342), system flags (lines 347-483)
     - Key format: `VAR_GLOBAL\t_00_IN00\tBOOL\t%IX0.0.0\t...\t입력 접점 00`
   
   - `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\SHINJIN\1 CELL 조립 장비\PLC RAW\ST_XEC\GVL_IO.st`
     - Originally contained IX_/QX_ named variables with AT% bindings and P-address comments
     - Was rewritten to be a reference-only comment file (no VAR_GLOBAL block) since XG5000 auto-registers all I/O variables
   
   - All 17 ST program files modified (ST_01_MAIN.st through ST_20_불량배출.st):
     - ST_01_MAIN.st: 17 replacements (analog module U0A vars, F00093, P0000B)
     - ST_02_HMI.st: 130 replacements (mostly F00093→_T1S used for 1-second blink clock)
     - ST_03_INPUT.st: 251 replacements (heaviest - all sensor input P-addresses)
     - ST_04_OUTPUT.st: 220 replacements (all solenoid/actuator output P-addresses)
     - ST_05_ALARM.st: 10 replacements (P00057, P0000B, P00095)
     - ST_06_SERVO.st: 13 replacements (P0000F, P00056, F0009A)
     - ST_10~ST_20: Various counts totaling remaining replacements
   
   - `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\rename_vars.py`
     - Python script created to perform bulk renaming
     - Contains complete mapping dictionaries: p_mapping (186 entries), f_mapping (3 entries), u0a_mapping (7 entries)
     - Uses regex word-boundary matching to avoid partial replacements
     - Sorts keys by length descending to prevent substring conflicts
     - Includes verification pass checking for remaining P/F/U0A patterns

4. Errors and fixes:
   - First attempt to run Python script via bash heredoc failed with "unexpected EOF while looking for matching `'`" error
     - Fix: Wrote the Python script to a file (`C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\rename_vars.py`) using the Write tool instead of inline heredoc, then executed it with `python rename_vars.py`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Successfully built the P-address to XG5000 name mapping by cross-referencing GVL_IO.st comments (which contained P-address annotations) with VAR.txt (which contained XG5000 names and AT% addresses)
   - The mapping required understanding the XBC slot-based addressing scheme: each slot has 32 bits (2 words of 16 bits), with P-address offsets following hex patterns within each slot range
   - Verification confirmed zero remaining P-address, F-address, or U0A patterns in any ST file after renaming
   - Total: 1041 replacements across 17 files

6. All user messages:
   - Single detailed task message with 6 steps: "Task: Rename ALL I/O and system variable names in ST files to match XG5000 auto-generated names" containing comprehensive instructions for building mapping table, mapping XBC P addresses, mapping special variables, applying renames, updating GVL_IO.st, and verifying results.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None explicitly remaining. The task was completed:
     - All P/F/U0A variables renamed (1041 replacements, 0 remaining)
     - GVL_IO.st simplified to reference-only comment file
     - Verification passed with no remaining old-style patterns

8. Current Work:
   The task was fully completed immediately before the summary request. The final actions were:
   - Running the rename script: 1041 replacements across 17 ST files
   - Verification: zero remaining P-address, F-address, or U0A patterns
   - Rewriting GVL_IO.st to a reference-only comment file explaining that all I/O variables are auto-registered by XG5000

9. Optional Next Step:
   No immediate next step required - the rename task is complete. If continuing, the user might want to:
   - Verify the renamed files compile correctly in XG5000
   - Clean up the temporary rename_vars.py script from the Desktop
   - Update any CLAUDE.md project documentation to reflect the completed variable rename
</summary>
